context: Evaluate a Bun.serve HTTP server implementation for correct API patterns and Bun-native usage

checklist.name: Bun.serve() used as the server primitive
checklist.description: The file uses Bun.serve({ fetch(req) { ... } }) as the entry point; no import from 'node:http', Express, Hono, or other frameworks

checklist.name: PORT from environment variable
checklist.description: The server port uses process.env.PORT or Bun.env.PORT with a fallback of 3000; port is not hardcoded

checklist.name: All four routes implemented
checklist.description: GET /tasks, POST /tasks, GET /tasks/:id, and DELETE /tasks/:id are all handled with appropriate HTTP status codes (200, 201, 404)

checklist.name: POST body parsed correctly
checklist.description: POST /tasks parses the JSON body using await req.json() (Bun-native Request API); does not use body-parser or stream concatenation

checklist.name: 404 fallback handler
checklist.description: Any unmatched route returns a Response with status 404 and a JSON body { error: 'not found' } or similar

checklist.name: TypeScript types used
checklist.description: The file uses TypeScript; task data structure has typed fields (at minimum title: string, done: boolean, id); no use of any as a shortcut for task shape
